patch.value: Context: Cloudflare returns error 1010 (body too large) for events over ~1MB. Current summarizeResult() limits arrays to 5 samples and objects to 25 keys, but nested values are unbounded. The 130MB queue with 70-retry entries needs both a fix and a cleanup. Recommendation: Option 3 (pre-enqueue size guard) is simplest and most robust - serialize the event, check byte length, and truncate result_summary progressively if over threshold. Also add a gc sub-command or health remediation to prune stuck queue entries.
Decision: Cap telemetry result_summary payload size

Decision needed: how to prevent telemetry events from exceeding proxy/CDN body size limits. Options: (1) Cap total serialized size of result_summary to 100KB, (2) Truncate sample values to max 1KB each, (3) Add pre-enqueue size guard that drops result_summary if event exceeds 500KB, (4) Reduce array sample limit from 5 to 2 and object key limit from 25 to 10.
Decision documented with rationale and implementation plan

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Prefer option 3 (pre-enqueue size guard) because it handles all edge cases without changing the capture fidelity for small/medium commands.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Telemetry payloads should always be bounded by total serialized size,not just array/object element counts.

patch.value: Decided: option 3 (pre-enqueue size guard at 512KB) combined with depth-limited sanitization (max depth 6) and max-attempts pruning (15). Implemented and verified with all 1087 tests passing.
